title: QNB Egypt (Egypt) Profile & Reviews
description: Explore a detailed profile and institutional data for QNB Egypt in Egypt. Share your experience or access institutional-grade data for professional insights.

faq.question: What is QNB Egypt?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt (Qatar National Bank S.A.E), formerly known as QNB Al Ahli, is one of Egypt's largest private sector banks and a subsidiary of QNB Group, the largest financial institution in the Middle East and Africa. The bank provides comprehensive retail, corporate, and investment banking services to over 1.7 million clients across Egypt. With a network of approximately 234 branches, 941 ATMs, and over 4,800 banking professionals, QNB Egypt is a major player in the Egyptian banking sector, offering world-class financial solutions to individuals and businesses.

faq.question: When was QNB Egypt founded?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt was originally established in April 1978 as a joint venture between QNB Group and Egyptian shareholders. The bank underwent significant transformation in 2013 when QNB Group acquired what was then Egypt's largest private-sector bank, National Société Générale Bank (NSGB), from Société Générale. Following the acquisition, the bank was rebranded as QNB Al Ahli. In 2024, QNB Group streamlined its brand identity across key overseas markets, phasing out the "Al Ahli" brand name and consolidating operations under the unified QNB Egypt brand. This evolution reflects the bank's journey from a joint venture to becoming a fully-owned subsidiary of QNB Group, one of the region's most prominent financial institutions.

faq.question: Where is QNB Egypt headquartered?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt is headquartered in Cairo, with its main office located at 5 Champollion Street, next to Steigenberger El Tahrir Hotel in Downtown Cairo. The headquarters can be reached at telephone number (02) 27707000. The bank maintains significant presence throughout Cairo and other major Egyptian cities, with strategically located branches serving key business districts and residential areas.

faq.question: Where does QNB Egypt operate?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt operates nationwide across Egypt with an extensive network of 234 branches serving major cities and regions including: Cairo: Multiple branches in Downtown, Nasr City, Dokki, Mohandessin, and other districts including Qasr El Nil, Talaat Harb, and Manial Palace locations. Alexandria: Branches in key areas such as Laurent, Mostafa Kamel, and other strategic locations along the Mediterranean coast. Giza: Comprehensive coverage including Haram, Faysal Street, and Al-Bahr Al-Azam areas. Upper Egypt: Branches in Fayoum, Beni Suef, Sohag, and Qena serving southern regions. Red Sea Resorts: Presence in tourist destinations including Sharm El Sheikh (Naama Bay and Nabq) and Hurghada. Suez Canal Zone: Branches in Port Said, Suez, and other canal cities. New Cities: Coverage in emerging urban areas including 6th of October City and El Shorouk City. The bank's 941 ATMs provide 24/7 banking access across the country. Customer service is available through the call center at 19700.

faq.question: What services does QNB Egypt offer?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt provides a comprehensive range of banking and financial services: Personal Banking: Savings and current accounts, personal loans, credit cards, debit cards, IBAN services, and consumer finance products tailored to individual needs. Business Banking: Business checking and savings accounts, commercial loans, overdraft facilities, supply chain finance, and working capital solutions for businesses of all sizes. Corporate Banking: Treasury management, cash management solutions, structured financing, project finance, and corporate lending for large enterprises. Trade Finance: Letters of credit, documentary collections, import/export financing, and international trade services. International Banking: International transfers, foreign exchange services, SWIFT transactions, and correspondent banking leveraging QNB Group's global network. Islamic Banking: Sharia-compliant banking products and services for customers seeking Islamic finance solutions. Digital Banking: Online banking platforms, mobile banking applications, and digital payment solutions providing convenient 24/7 account access. Investment Services: Wealth management, investment advisory, and asset management services for high-net-worth individuals and institutional clients.

faq.question: What makes QNB Egypt unique?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt stands out as Egypt's second-largest private sector bank, backed by the financial strength of QNB Group, which reported total assets of approximately US$327 billion as of 2022. This provides exceptional stability and resources to serve Egyptian customers. The bank's extensive branch network of 234 locations makes it one of the most accessible banks in Egypt, with particularly strong presence in Cairo, Alexandria, and major urban centers. The 941 ATMs ensure customers have convenient access to banking services nationwide. QNB Egypt's 2013 acquisition and integration of National Société Générale Bank represented one of the largest banking deals in Egypt's history, demonstrating QNB Group's commitment to the Egyptian market. The bank serves over 1.7 million clients with a team of more than 4,800 banking professionals. The bank's competitive exchange rates, fast international transfer times, and comprehensive digital banking platforms make it a preferred choice for both domestic and international banking needs. Its connection to QNB Group's network across 31 countries facilitates seamless international transactions and trade finance.

faq.question: What type of financial institution is QNB Egypt?
faq.answer: QNB Egypt is a universal bank (Arabic: بنك شامل) operating as a joint-stock company (S.A.E - Société Anonyme Egyptienne) under the supervision of the Central Bank of Egypt. As a universal bank, it provides a full spectrum of banking services including retail banking, corporate banking, investment banking, and wealth management. The institution is registered with the Central Bank of Egypt and operates under Egyptian banking regulations while maintaining the international standards and best practices of its parent company, QNB Group. This structure allows it to serve diverse market segments from individual consumers to large corporations while benefiting from the resources and expertise of one of the region's largest financial institutions.
